message:
test(db,share): add extract_ident_after_keyword and read_chunked_body tests (br-3h13.17.3, br-3h13.17.5)

11 tests for extract_ident_after_keyword covering CREATE TABLE/INDEX/TRIGGER/VIRTUAL TABLE,
case insensitivity, empty SQL, keyword-at-end, multiple spaces, underscores, and
parenthesis-abutting identifiers.

8 tests for read_chunked_body covering single/multiple chunks, empty body, hex
upper/lowercase, chunk extensions, invalid hex error, and binary data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1099,12 +1099,12 @@
 {"id":"br-3h13.16.6.1","title":"T16.6.1: E2E script reproducing case-duplicate doom loop and verifying fix","description":"E2E script: reproduce the case-duplicate agent doom loop and verify the fix.

Script: tests/e2e/test_doom_loop_case_duplicate.sh (or similar)

Steps:
1. Build am CLI (cargo build -p mcp-agent-mail-cli)
2. Create a fresh SQLite DB in tempdir
3. Manually apply migrations v1-v9 (using am internal schema or raw SQL)
4. INSERT case-duplicate agents: ("SilverFox", project=1, id=1) and ("silverfox", project=1, id=2) and ("SILVERFOX", project=1, id=3)
5. Also insert a valid project row
6. Set DATABASE_URL to point to this DB
7. Run: am agents list --project <project_slug>
   - EXPECTED: succeeds (v10a dedup runs on first connection, v10b creates index)
   - Log full stdout/stderr
8. Verify DB state:
   - Only 1 agent remains for project=1 (the one with id=1)
   - Index idx_agents_project_name_nocase exists
9. Run: am agents list --project <project_slug> AGAIN
   - EXPECTED: succeeds (idempotent, migration already applied)
10. Run: am send --project <project_slug> --from "NewAgent" --to "SilverFox" --subject "test" --body "hello"
    - EXPECTED: succeeds (SilverFox still exists as the deduped survivor)

Logging:
- set -euo pipefail at top
- echo each step with timestamp
- Save all command outputs to artifacts/ directory
- On failure: dump DB schema (sqlite3 .schema), dump agents table, dump schema_migrations table
- Exit with clear pass/fail status

File: tests/e2e/test_doom_loop_case_duplicate.sh","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldChapel","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:37:15.587823964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:35:20.386029395Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T06:35:20.386005410Z","close_reason":"E2E case_duplicate_dedup: 5 assertions (pre-v10 DB with 3 case-dup agents, v10a dedup to 1 agent, survivor=SilverFox, NOCASE index created, idempotent)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.16.6.1","depends_on_id":"br-3h13.16.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:37:29.330712715Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3h13.16.6.1","depends_on_id":"br-3h13.16.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:37:15.587823964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-3h13.16.6.2","title":"T16.6.2: E2E script for doctor check/repair lifecycle on broken DB","description":"E2E script: doctor check + doctor repair full lifecycle on a broken DB.

Script: tests/e2e/test_doctor_doom_loop_lifecycle.sh (or similar)

Steps:
1. Build am CLI
2. Create a SQLite DB at v9 schema with case-duplicate agents (doom loop trigger)
3. Set DATABASE_URL

Phase A — Doctor Check on broken DB:
4. Run: am doctor check
5. EXPECTED: doctor reports failures for db_file_sanity or pool_init (the migration will fail)
6. Log and save full output

Phase B — Doctor Repair:
7. Run: am doctor repair
8. EXPECTED: repair creates backups (both timestamped in backups/ AND .bak sibling)
9. Verify .bak sibling file exists and is valid SQLite
10. Verify backups/ directory contains timestamped backup

Phase C — Verify recovery:
11. Run: am doctor check AGAIN
12. EXPECTED: now passes (repair fixed the DB or the next connection init will)
13. Run: am agents list --project <slug>
14. EXPECTED: succeeds

Phase D — Simulate corruption recovery:
15. Corrupt the primary DB (write garbage to header)
16. Run: am agents list --project <slug>
17. EXPECTED: pool auto-recovery kicks in, finds .bak sibling, restores, command succeeds
18. (If auto-recovery is not triggered by CLI commands, just verify find_healthy_backup logic separately)

Logging:
- Detailed step-by-step logging with timestamps
- Save all outputs to artifacts/
- Dump file sizes, md5sums of DB and .bak at each phase
- On failure: dump full directory listing, DB state, error logs

 {"id":"br-3h13.17","title":"[track] T17: Unit Test Completion — Pure Functions (No DB, No Mocks)","description":"Cover all 20 untested pure/sync functions identified by comprehensive audit (2026-02-17). Every function listed below is exercisable without DB setup, async runtime, or external services — just construct inputs and assert outputs. Grouped by crate for efficient batching: UTF-8 handling (3 functions), path/glob operations (5), SQL schema extraction (2), CLI utilities (2), scrub/share utilities (4), and metrics/search utilities (4). All tests MUST use real implementations, no mocks.

Baseline: 4,092+ workspace tests. Target: +55-70 tests covering all 20 functions with boundary, error, and multi-byte edge cases.

Dependency structure: All 6 leaf tasks are independent (no inter-task deps). Can be fully parallelized across agents.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"track","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:46:24.939581435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T15:04:52.413202120Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T15:04:52.413133562Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing","unit-test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.17","depends_on_id":"br-3h13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:46:24.939581435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":794,"issue_id":"br-3h13.17","author":"RubyPrairie","text":"Audit findings (2026-02-17): Comprehensive code search found 20 untested pure/sync functions across 7 crates. All are testable without DB, async runtime, or external services. Grouped into 6 leaf tasks by crate/domain for maximum parallelism. Priority ranking: (HIGH) UTF-8 boundary handling (truncate_utf8, push_utf8_char) and path security (validate_archive_component) — bugs here cause data corruption or security holes. (MEDIUM) SQL extraction (extract_ident_after_keyword, extract_trigger_statements) — bugs skip migrations silently. CLI formatting (format_age, classify_stderr) — bugs confuse users. (LOWER) Saturation arithmetic (i64_from_u64_saturating) — bugs cause metric overflow. Code snippet extraction (backtick_snippets) — search quality impact only. Total estimated: 76-99 new tests across all 6 tasks.","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:50:17Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-3h13.17.1","title":"T17.1: UTF-8 handling tests — utf8_char_width, push_utf8_char, truncate_utf8","description":"Add unit tests for 3 UTF-8 helper functions that handle multi-byte character boundaries. These are correctness-critical — a bug here silently corrupts text or panics on slice boundaries.

FUNCTIONS TO TEST:

1. utf8_char_width(byte: u8) -> usize (crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs:2329)
   - const fn that returns 1/2/3/4 based on leading byte pattern
   - Tests needed: ASCII (0x00-0x7F→1), 2-byte (0xC0-0xDF→2), 3-byte (0xE0-0xEF→3), 4-byte (0xF0-0xFF→4)
   - Edge cases: 0x7F (max ASCII), 0x80 (continuation byte→should return 1), 0xC0 (min 2-byte), 0xF4 (max valid 4-byte)

2. push_utf8_char(buf: &mut Vec<u8>, bytes: &[u8], pos: usize) -> usize (queries.rs:2347)
   - Pushes a complete UTF-8 character from bytes at position pos into buf, returns bytes consumed
   - Tests needed: ASCII char, 2-byte (e.g. ñ 0xC3 0xB1), 3-byte (e.g. € 0xE2 0x82 0xAC), 4-byte (e.g. 𝄞 0xF0 0x9D 0x84 0x9E)
   - Edge cases: pos at end of slice, truncated multi-byte sequence, empty slice

3. truncate_utf8(s: &str, max_bytes: usize) -> &str (crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs:3263)
   - Safely truncates string to max_bytes without breaking UTF-8 char boundaries
   - Tests needed: ASCII-only string, string with multi-byte chars at boundary, max_bytes=0, max_bytes > len (no-op), max_bytes lands mid-character (should back up to previous char boundary)
   - Edge cases: string of all 4-byte emoji, empty string, max_bytes=1 on multi-byte-only string

TARGET: 12-15 tests. Estimated effort: 30 minutes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeCave","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:46:42.173691184Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T14:39:41.657002052Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T14:39:27.665562392Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing","unit-test","utf8"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.17.1","depends_on_id":"br-3h13.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:46:42.173691184Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":800,"issue_id":"br-3h13.17.1","author":"JadeCave","text":"JadeCave claiming T17.1 (UTF-8 handling tests).","created_at":"2026-02-18T14:17:23Z"},{"id":803,"issue_id":"br-3h13.17.1","author":"JadeCave","text":"Done: 19 tests across 2 crates. queries.rs: 8 tests (utf8_char_width ASCII/multibyte/known chars, push_utf8_char ASCII/2/3/4-byte + sequential + boundary). storage/lib.rs: 11 tests (truncate_utf8 within limit, boundary, empty, zero max, multibyte boundary, 3/4-byte, emoji, greater than length, multibyte-only). All passing.","created_at":"2026-02-18T14:39:41Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-3h13.17.2","title":"T17.2: Path/glob operation tests — normalize_lexical_path, resolve_search_root, has_glob_tokens, golden_matches_filter, validate_archive_component","description":"Add unit tests for 5 path/glob utility functions. validate_archive_component is security-relevant (path traversal prevention).

FUNCTIONS TO TEST:

1. normalize_lexical_path(path: &Path) -> PathBuf (crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/legacy.rs:1441)
   - Filters '.' and '..' components lexically (no filesystem access)
   - Tests: simple path, path with '.', path with '..', 'a/../b' -> 'b', leading '..', trailing '/', empty path
   - Edge cases: all-dots path, deeply nested '../../../', Windows-style separators

2. resolve_search_root(root: Option<&str>) -> PathBuf (crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/legacy.rs:1421)
   - Returns given path or falls back to current directory
   - Tests: Some(valid_path), Some(relative_path), None (uses cwd)
   - NOTE: Uses std::env::current_dir() so test in tempdir with known layout

3. has_glob_tokens(s: &str) -> bool (crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs:471)
   - Simple check for '*', '?', '[' characters
   - Tests: 'hello' -> false, 'hello*' -> true, 'fo?' -> true, 'items[0]' -> true, '' -> false, '\*' -> true (escaped but char still present)

4. golden_matches_filter(name: &str, filter: Option<&str>) -> bool (crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs:3353)
   - Matches filename against optional glob pattern; None filter matches everything
   - Tests: None filter -> true, Some('*.json') vs 'test.json' -> true, Some('*.json') vs 'test.txt' -> false, case sensitivity
   - Edge cases: empty filter string, filter with path separators

5. validate_archive_component(component: &str) -> Result<()> (crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs:3274)
   - SECURITY: Validates path components are safe (not empty, not '.' or '..', no separators, no NUL)
   - Tests: valid name, empty string -> Err, '.' -> Err, '..' -> Err, 'a/b' -> Err, 'a\0b' -> Err, 'normal_name' -> Ok
   - Edge cases: very long component, unicode filename, whitespace-only, component starting with '-'

TARGET: 18-22 tests. Estimated effort: 45 minutes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeCave","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:46:58.211478712Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T14:39:41.934315417Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T14:39:27.951925712Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["path","testing","unit-test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.17.2","depends_on_id":"br-3h13.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:46:58.211478712Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":801,"issue_id":"br-3h13.17.2","author":"JadeCave","text":"JadeCave claiming T17.2 (path/glob tests).","created_at":"2026-02-18T14:17:23Z"},{"id":804,"issue_id":"br-3h13.17.2","author":"JadeCave","text":"Done: 22 tests across 4 files. legacy.rs: 11 tests (normalize_lexical_path simple/curdir/parent/deeply nested/leading parent/absolute/all dots/root/parent past root, resolve_search_root with and without path). ci.rs: 5 tests (has_glob_tokens plain/star/question/bracket/escaped). lib.rs: 5 tests (golden_matches_filter none/glob/exact/wildcard/question). storage/lib.rs: 8 tests (validate_archive_component valid/empty/dot/separators/nul/whitespace/unicode/dash). All passing.","created_at":"2026-02-18T14:39:41Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-3h13.17.3","title":"T17.3: SQL schema extraction tests — extract_ident_after_keyword, extract_trigger_statements","description":"Add unit tests for 2 SQL parsing functions used in schema migration processing. Correctness matters because wrong extraction silently skips migrations.

FUNCTIONS TO TEST:

1. extract_ident_after_keyword(sql: &str, keyword: &str) -> Option<String> (crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/schema.rs:324)
   - Extracts SQL identifier after a keyword (e.g. table name after 'CREATE TABLE')
   - Tests: 'CREATE TABLE foo (id INT)' with keyword 'TABLE' -> Some('foo')
   - 'CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bar (id INT)' -> Some('bar') (skips IF NOT EXISTS)
   - 'CREATE INDEX idx ON table_name' with keyword 'INDEX' -> Some('idx')
   - Quoted identifiers: 'CREATE TABLE "my-table"' -> Some('my-table')
   - Backtick identifiers: 'CREATE TABLE `my_table`'
   - Schema-qualified: 'CREATE TABLE main.foo' -> Some('foo') or 'main.foo'
   - Edge cases: empty SQL, keyword not found, keyword at end of string, multiple spaces between keyword and ident

2. extract_trigger_statements(sql: &str) -> Vec<String> (crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/schema.rs:373)
   - Extracts complete CREATE TRIGGER statements from a multi-statement SQL string
   - Tests: single trigger, multiple triggers in one string, trigger with BEGIN...END body
   - Mixed content: triggers interleaved with CREATE TABLE/INDEX statements
   - Edge cases: empty string -> empty vec, no triggers -> empty vec, nested semicolons inside trigger body
   - Trigger with complex body: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE inside BEGIN...END

TARGET: 10-14 tests. Estimated effort: 30 minutes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeCave","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:47:10.125742726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T14:39:42.216767402Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T14:39:28.250130717Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["schema","sql","testing","unit-test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.17.3","depends_on_id":"br-3h13.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:47:10.125742726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":802,"issue_id":"br-3h13.17.3","author":"JadeCave","text":"JadeCave claiming T17.3 (SQL schema extraction tests).","created_at":"2026-02-18T14:17:24Z"},{"id":805,"issue_id":"br-3h13.17.3","author":"JadeCave","text":"Done: 18 tests. extract_ident_after_keyword: 10 tests (table/index/trigger/virtual table/not found/empty/keyword at end/case insensitive/multiple spaces/underscore name). extract_trigger_statements: 5 tests (single/multiple/empty/no triggers/mixed). derive_migration_id: 3 tests (table/index/unknown). All passing.","created_at":"2026-02-18T14:39:42Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-3h13.17.4","title":"T17.4: CLI utility tests — format_age, classify_stderr","description":"Add unit tests for 2 CLI utility functions. format_age is user-visible (wrong output = confusing UX). classify_stderr affects CI pipeline interpretation.

FUNCTIONS TO TEST:

1. format_age(seconds: i64) -> String (crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/robot.rs:810)
   - Converts seconds into human-readable relative time ('just now', '5 minutes ago', '2 hours ago', etc.)
   - Tests needed for each time bucket:
     - 0 seconds -> 'just now' (or '0s')
     - 30 seconds -> '30s ago' or 'just now'
     - 60 seconds -> '1 minute ago'
     - 3600 seconds -> '1 hour ago'
     - 86400 seconds -> '1 day ago'
     - 604800 seconds -> '1 week ago' or '7 days ago'
     - Negative seconds (future timestamp): should handle gracefully
   - Edge cases: very large values (years), boundary between buckets (59s vs 60s, 3599s vs 3600s)

2. classify_stderr(stderr: &str) -> StderrKind (crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs:325)
   - Classifies stderr output for CI diagnostic purposes
   - Tests for each classification:
     - Empty stderr -> Clean/None
     - Compiler error patterns ('error[E0xxx]:', 'cannot find')
     - Test failure patterns ('test result: FAILED', 'panicked at')
     - Warning patterns ('warning:', 'unused variable')
     - Link error patterns ('undefined reference', 'ld: ')
     - Mixed output: errors + warnings -> should classify as error (highest severity)
   - Edge cases: very long stderr, binary garbage in stderr, stderr with ANSI color codes

TARGET: 10-14 tests. Estimated effort: 30 minutes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeCave","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:47:23.212376845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T15:04:44.811503149Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T15:04:44.811432256Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","testing","unit-test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.17.4","depends_on_id":"br-3h13.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:47:23.212376845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-3h13.17.5","title":"T17.5: Scrub/share utility tests — normalize_redact_key, is_empty_value, build_placeholders (scope.rs), read_chunked_body","description":"Add unit tests for 4 functions in the share crate. normalize_redact_key and is_empty_value are security-adjacent (scrubbing sensitive data). read_chunked_body handles HTTP transfer encoding.

FUNCTIONS TO TEST:

1. normalize_redact_key(key: &str) -> String (crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/scrub.rs:58)
   - Normalizes sensitive key names before redaction matching (lowercase, strip whitespace/NUL, etc.)
   - Tests: 'API_KEY' -> 'api_key', '  Secret  ' -> 'secret', 'pass\0word' -> 'password'
   - Mixed case, hyphens vs underscores, Unicode characters, empty string
   - Verify idempotence: normalize(normalize(x)) == normalize(x)

2. is_empty_value(value: &serde_json::Value) -> bool (crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/scrub.rs:456)
   - Checks if JSON values are 'empty' (null, empty string, empty array/object)
   - Tests: Value::Null -> true, Value::String('') -> true, Value::String('x') -> false
   - Value::Array([]) -> true, Value::Array([1]) -> false
   - Value::Object({}) -> true, Value::Object({k:v}) -> false
   - Value::Bool(false) -> false (not empty), Value::Number(0) -> false (not empty)

3. build_placeholders(count: usize) -> String (crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/scope.rs:293)
   - Builds SQL placeholder string '?,?,?' for given count
   - Tests: 0 -> '' (empty), 1 -> '?', 3 -> '?,?,?', large count (100)
   - NOTE: Different from queries.rs build_placeholders which IS already tested

4. read_chunked_body(stream: &mut impl Read) -> Result<Vec<u8>> (crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/probe.rs:543)
   - Reads HTTP chunked transfer encoding body
   - Tests: single chunk, multiple chunks, empty body (0\r\n\r\n), chunk with extension
   - Edge cases: very large chunk size, invalid hex in chunk length, missing trailing CRLF
   - Can test with Cursor<Vec<u8>> (no network needed)

TARGET: 14-18 tests. Estimated effort: 45 minutes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeCave","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:47:38.397607305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T15:04:44.815003471Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T15:04:44.811432256Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["security","share","testing","unit-test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.17.5","depends_on_id":"br-3h13.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:47:38.397607305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-3h13.17.6","title":"T17.6: Metrics/search utility tests — i64_from_u64_saturating, elapsed_ms_saturating, value_as_i64, backtick_snippets","description":"Add unit tests for 4 numeric/utility functions across metrics and search modules. i64_from_u64_saturating handles the u64→i64 boundary which matters for tool metrics reporting.

FUNCTIONS TO TEST:

1. i64_from_u64_saturating(val: u64) -> i64 (crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tool_metrics.rs:228)
   - Converts u64 to i64 with saturation at i64::MAX instead of wrapping
   - Tests: 0u64 -> 0i64, 42u64 -> 42i64, i64::MAX as u64 -> i64::MAX
   - u64::MAX -> i64::MAX (saturated), (i64::MAX as u64) + 1 -> i64::MAX (boundary)
   - Verify NO wrapping to negative values

2. elapsed_ms_saturating(start: Instant) -> u64 (crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/consistency.rs:161)
   - Converts Instant elapsed duration to saturating u64 milliseconds
   - Tests: recent instant -> small value, instant from 100ms ago -> ~100
   - Cannot test overflow easily but verify monotonicity: later call >= earlier call
   - Verify: never panics, always returns >= 0

3. value_as_i64(value: &Value) -> Option<i64> (crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs:447)
   - Extracts i64 from sqlmodel Value variants
   - Tests: Value::BigInt(42) -> Some(42), Value::Int(7) -> Some(7)
   - Value::SmallInt(3) -> Some(3), Value::Null -> None
   - Value::String('hello') -> None (non-numeric types)
   - Edge cases: Value::BigInt(i64::MAX), Value::BigInt(i64::MIN), Value::BigInt(0)

4. backtick_snippets(text: &str) -> Vec<&str> (crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/search.rs:150)
   - Extracts backtick-delimited code snippets from text
   - Tests: 'use `foo` here' -> ['foo'], 'no backticks' -> [], '`a` and `b`' -> ['a', 'b']
   - Edge cases: empty string, unmatched backtick, adjacent backticks ``(empty), triple backticks
   - Nested: '`outer `inner` end`' — verify behavior

+{"id":"br-3h13.17.1","title":"T17.1: UTF-8 handling tests — utf8_char_width, push_utf8_char, truncate_utf8","description":"Add unit tests for 3 UTF-8 helper functions that handle multi-byte character boundaries. These are correctness-critical — a bug here silently corrupts text or panics on slice boundaries.

FUNCTIONS TO TEST:

1. utf8_char_width(byte: u8) -> usize (crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs:2329)
   - const fn that returns 1/2/3/4 based on leading byte pattern
   - Tests needed: ASCII (0x00-0x7F→1), 2-byte (0xC0-0xDF→2), 3-byte (0xE0-0xEF→3), 4-byte (0xF0-0xFF→4)
   - Edge cases: 0x7F (max ASCII), 0x80 (continuation byte→should return 1), 0xC0 (min 2-byte), 0xF4 (max valid 4-byte)

2. push_utf8_char(buf: &mut Vec<u8>, bytes: &[u8], pos: usize) -> usize (queries.rs:2347)
   - Pushes a complete UTF-8 character from bytes at position pos into buf, returns bytes consumed
   - Tests needed: ASCII char, 2-byte (e.g. ñ 0xC3 0xB1), 3-byte (e.g. € 0xE2 0x82 0xAC), 4-byte (e.g. 𝄞 0xF0 0x9D 0x84 0x9E)
   - Edge cases: pos at end of slice, truncated multi-byte sequence, empty slice

3. truncate_utf8(s: &str, max_bytes: usize) -> &str (crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs:3263)
   - Safely truncates string to max_bytes without breaking UTF-8 char boundaries
   - Tests needed: ASCII-only string, string with multi-byte chars at boundary, max_bytes=0, max_bytes > len (no-op), max_bytes lands mid-character (should back up to previous char boundary)
   - Edge cases: string of all 4-byte emoji, empty string, max_bytes=1 on multi-byte-only string

+{"id":"br-3h13.17.2","title":"T17.2: Path/glob operation tests — normalize_lexical_path, resolve_search_root, has_glob_tokens, golden_matches_filter, validate_archive_component","description":"Add unit tests for 5 path/glob utility functions. validate_archive_component is security-relevant (path traversal prevention).

FUNCTIONS TO TEST:

1. normalize_lexical_path(path: &Path) -> PathBuf (crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/legacy.rs:1441)
   - Filters '.' and '..' components lexically (no filesystem access)
   - Tests: simple path, path with '.', path with '..', 'a/../b' -> 'b', leading '..', trailing '/', empty path
   - Edge cases: all-dots path, deeply nested '../../../', Windows-style separators

2. resolve_search_root(root: Option<&str>) -> PathBuf (crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/legacy.rs:1421)
   - Returns given path or falls back to current directory
   - Tests: Some(valid_path), Some(relative_path), None (uses cwd)
   - NOTE: Uses std::env::current_dir() so test in tempdir with known layout

3. has_glob_tokens(s: &str) -> bool (crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs:471)
   - Simple check for '*', '?', '[' characters
   - Tests: 'hello' -> false, 'hello*' -> true, 'fo?' -> true, 'items[0]' -> true, '' -> false, '\*' -> true (escaped but char still present)

4. golden_matches_filter(name: &str, filter: Option<&str>) -> bool (crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs:3353)
   - Matches filename against optional glob pattern; None filter matches everything
   - Tests: None filter -> true, Some('*.json') vs 'test.json' -> true, Some('*.json') vs 'test.txt' -> false, case sensitivity
   - Edge cases: empty filter string, filter with path separators

5. validate_archive_component(component: &str) -> Result<()> (crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs:3274)
   - SECURITY: Validates path components are safe (not empty, not '.' or '..', no separators, no NUL)
   - Tests: valid name, empty string -> Err, '.' -> Err, '..' -> Err, 'a/b' -> Err, 'a\0b' -> Err, 'normal_name' -> Ok
   - Edge cases: very long component, unicode filename, whitespace-only, component starting with '-'

+{"id":"br-3h13.17.3","title":"T17.3: SQL schema extraction tests — extract_ident_after_keyword, extract_trigger_statements","description":"Add unit tests for 2 SQL parsing functions used in schema migration processing. Correctness matters because wrong extraction silently skips migrations.

FUNCTIONS TO TEST:

1. extract_ident_after_keyword(sql: &str, keyword: &str) -> Option<String> (crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/schema.rs:324)
   - Extracts SQL identifier after a keyword (e.g. table name after 'CREATE TABLE')
   - Tests: 'CREATE TABLE foo (id INT)' with keyword 'TABLE' -> Some('foo')
   - 'CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bar (id INT)' -> Some('bar') (skips IF NOT EXISTS)
   - 'CREATE INDEX idx ON table_name' with keyword 'INDEX' -> Some('idx')
   - Quoted identifiers: 'CREATE TABLE "my-table"' -> Some('my-table')
   - Backtick identifiers: 'CREATE TABLE `my_table`'
   - Schema-qualified: 'CREATE TABLE main.foo' -> Some('foo') or 'main.foo'
   - Edge cases: empty SQL, keyword not found, keyword at end of string, multiple spaces between keyword and ident

2. extract_trigger_statements(sql: &str) -> Vec<String> (crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/schema.rs:373)
   - Extracts complete CREATE TRIGGER statements from a multi-statement SQL string
   - Tests: single trigger, multiple triggers in one string, trigger with BEGIN...END body
   - Mixed content: triggers interleaved with CREATE TABLE/INDEX statements
   - Edge cases: empty string -> empty vec, no triggers -> empty vec, nested semicolons inside trigger body
   - Trigger with complex body: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE inside BEGIN...END

+{"id":"br-3h13.17.4","title":"T17.4: CLI utility tests — format_age, classify_stderr","description":"Add unit tests for 2 CLI utility functions. format_age is user-visible (wrong output = confusing UX). classify_stderr affects CI pipeline interpretation.

FUNCTIONS TO TEST:

1. format_age(seconds: i64) -> String (crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/robot.rs:810)
   - Converts seconds into human-readable relative time ('just now', '5 minutes ago', '2 hours ago', etc.)
   - Tests needed for each time bucket:
     - 0 seconds -> 'just now' (or '0s')
     - 30 seconds -> '30s ago' or 'just now'
     - 60 seconds -> '1 minute ago'
     - 3600 seconds -> '1 hour ago'
     - 86400 seconds -> '1 day ago'
     - 604800 seconds -> '1 week ago' or '7 days ago'
     - Negative seconds (future timestamp): should handle gracefully
   - Edge cases: very large values (years), boundary between buckets (59s vs 60s, 3599s vs 3600s)

2. classify_stderr(stderr: &str) -> StderrKind (crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs:325)
   - Classifies stderr output for CI diagnostic purposes
   - Tests for each classification:
     - Empty stderr -> Clean/None
     - Compiler error patterns ('error[E0xxx]:', 'cannot find')
     - Test failure patterns ('test result: FAILED', 'panicked at')
     - Warning patterns ('warning:', 'unused variable')
     - Link error patterns ('undefined reference', 'ld: ')
     - Mixed output: errors + warnings -> should classify as error (highest severity)
   - Edge cases: very long stderr, binary garbage in stderr, stderr with ANSI color codes

+{"id":"br-3h13.17.5","title":"T17.5: Scrub/share utility tests — normalize_redact_key, is_empty_value, build_placeholders (scope.rs), read_chunked_body","description":"Add unit tests for 4 functions in the share crate. normalize_redact_key and is_empty_value are security-adjacent (scrubbing sensitive data). read_chunked_body handles HTTP transfer encoding.

FUNCTIONS TO TEST:

1. normalize_redact_key(key: &str) -> String (crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/scrub.rs:58)
   - Normalizes sensitive key names before redaction matching (lowercase, strip whitespace/NUL, etc.)
   - Tests: 'API_KEY' -> 'api_key', '  Secret  ' -> 'secret', 'pass\0word' -> 'password'
   - Mixed case, hyphens vs underscores, Unicode characters, empty string
   - Verify idempotence: normalize(normalize(x)) == normalize(x)

2. is_empty_value(value: &serde_json::Value) -> bool (crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/scrub.rs:456)
   - Checks if JSON values are 'empty' (null, empty string, empty array/object)
   - Tests: Value::Null -> true, Value::String('') -> true, Value::String('x') -> false
   - Value::Array([]) -> true, Value::Array([1]) -> false
   - Value::Object({}) -> true, Value::Object({k:v}) -> false
   - Value::Bool(false) -> false (not empty), Value::Number(0) -> false (not empty)

3. build_placeholders(count: usize) -> String (crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/scope.rs:293)
   - Builds SQL placeholder string '?,?,?' for given count
   - Tests: 0 -> '' (empty), 1 -> '?', 3 -> '?,?,?', large count (100)
   - NOTE: Different from queries.rs build_placeholders which IS already tested

4. read_chunked_body(stream: &mut impl Read) -> Result<Vec<u8>> (crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/probe.rs:543)
   - Reads HTTP chunked transfer encoding body
   - Tests: single chunk, multiple chunks, empty body (0\r\n\r\n), chunk with extension
   - Edge cases: very large chunk size, invalid hex in chunk length, missing trailing CRLF
   - Can test with Cursor<Vec<u8>> (no network needed)

+{"id":"br-3h13.17.6","title":"T17.6: Metrics/search utility tests — i64_from_u64_saturating, elapsed_ms_saturating, value_as_i64, backtick_snippets","description":"Add unit tests for 4 numeric/utility functions across metrics and search modules. i64_from_u64_saturating handles the u64→i64 boundary which matters for tool metrics reporting.

FUNCTIONS TO TEST:

1. i64_from_u64_saturating(val: u64) -> i64 (crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tool_metrics.rs:228)
   - Converts u64 to i64 with saturation at i64::MAX instead of wrapping
   - Tests: 0u64 -> 0i64, 42u64 -> 42i64, i64::MAX as u64 -> i64::MAX
   - u64::MAX -> i64::MAX (saturated), (i64::MAX as u64) + 1 -> i64::MAX (boundary)
   - Verify NO wrapping to negative values

2. elapsed_ms_saturating(start: Instant) -> u64 (crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/consistency.rs:161)
   - Converts Instant elapsed duration to saturating u64 milliseconds
   - Tests: recent instant -> small value, instant from 100ms ago -> ~100
   - Cannot test overflow easily but verify monotonicity: later call >= earlier call
   - Verify: never panics, always returns >= 0

3. value_as_i64(value: &Value) -> Option<i64> (crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs:447)
   - Extracts i64 from sqlmodel Value variants
   - Tests: Value::BigInt(42) -> Some(42), Value::Int(7) -> Some(7)
   - Value::SmallInt(3) -> Some(3), Value::Null -> None
   - Value::String('hello') -> None (non-numeric types)
   - Edge cases: Value::BigInt(i64::MAX), Value::BigInt(i64::MIN), Value::BigInt(0)

4. backtick_snippets(text: &str) -> Vec<&str> (crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/search.rs:150)
   - Extracts backtick-delimited code snippets from text
   - Tests: 'use `foo` here' -> ['foo'], 'no backticks' -> [], '`a` and `b`' -> ['a', 'b']
   - Edge cases: empty string, unmatched backtick, adjacent backticks ``(empty), triple backticks
   - Nested: '`outer `inner` end`' — verify behavior

 {"id":"br-3h13.18","title":"[track] T18: E2E Gap Closure — Remaining Coverage Holes","description":"Close the 7 remaining E2E coverage gaps identified by comprehensive audit (2026-02-17). The project has 103 E2E test files and 10 of the original 15 gaps have been addressed. These 7 gaps represent the final missing scenarios.

Gaps to close:
1. resource://project/{slug} + agents/{slug} — no dedicated E2E scripts
2. Write-behind cache flush timer — no E2E verification of deferred-touch behavior
3. am robot against empty DB — no explicit empty-database behavioral testing
4. WORKTREES_ENABLED=false build slot behavior — only =true tested
5. Guard against live MCP-populated DB — guard tested in isolation only
6. --format md coverage — only thread tested explicitly with markdown
7. Stress test result correctness — operations verified as succeeding but data integrity not asserted

MANDATORY LOGGING STANDARD: Every new E2E test case MUST save full JSON-RPC request/response to artifacts/{case}/, include timing, and produce structured diagnostics on failure. Use e2e_lib.sh helpers (e2e_rpc_call, e2e_mark_case_start/end, e2e_case_banner).

Dependency structure: All 7 leaf tasks are independent except T18.5 (guard+MCP DB) which should run after T18.1 (resource tests create a populated DB pattern to reuse).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"track","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:48:11.718738739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T15:38:59.536705573Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T15:38:59.536684664Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.18","depends_on_id":"br-3h13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:48:11.718738739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":795,"issue_id":"br-3h13.18","author":"RubyPrairie","text":"Audit findings (2026-02-17): Of the original 15 E2E gaps identified, 10 have been fully addressed (tooling_resources, macro_prepare_thread, concurrent tests, HTTP transport, robot flags, null_fields/unicode, malformed_rpc, product bus coverage). 5 gaps remain open or partially addressed, expanded here into 7 leaf tasks (some gaps split into multiple tasks). Key risk areas: (1) Empty-DB robot behavior is the 'day zero' experience for every new agent session — broken empty-state handling = broken onboarding. (2) Guard+MCP integration is the only scenario that tests the full reservation→guard pipeline end-to-end through real MCP tools. (3) Stress correctness is the only way to catch lost writes, stale cache, and duplicate IDs under load. All tasks use mandatory e2e_lib.sh logging standard (request.json, response.json, headers.txt, timing.txt per case).","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:50:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-3h13.18.1","title":"T18.1: E2E test_resources_project_agents.sh — dedicated resource://project/{slug} + agents/{slug} coverage","description":"Create a new E2E script testing resource://project/{slug} and resource://agents/{slug} endpoints which currently have NO dedicated test scripts. These are fundamental resources used by every MCP client.

TEST CASES TO IMPLEMENT:

1. project_by_slug_happy_path
   - Create project via ensure_project, then fetch resource://project/{slug}
   - Assert: response contains project name, slug, created_at, agent count
   - Assert: JSON structure matches Python fixture format

2. project_by_slug_not_found
   - Fetch resource://project/nonexistent-slug
   - Assert: returns appropriate error (404 or error object)

3. project_by_slug_invalid_characters
   - Fetch resource://project/has spaces or resource://project/../../traversal
   - Assert: returns error, no path traversal

4. agents_by_slug_happy_path
   - Register 3 agents (RedFox, BlueLake, GoldHawk), fetch resource://agents/{slug}
   - Assert: response contains array of 3 agents with name, registered_at, status
   - Assert: agents sorted by name or registration order (match Python)

5. agents_by_slug_empty_project
   - Create project with no agents, fetch resource://agents/{slug}
   - Assert: returns empty array (not error)

6. agents_by_slug_not_found
   - Fetch resource://agents/nonexistent-slug
   - Assert: returns appropriate error

7. agents_by_slug_after_registration_change
   - Register agent, fetch agents (see 1), register another, fetch again (see 2)
   - Assert: second fetch shows 2 agents (live data, not cached stale)

LOGGING: Use e2e_lib.sh helpers. Save request.json, response.json, headers.txt, timing.txt per case. On failure, dump server logs.

 {"id":"br-3h13.18.2","title":"T18.2: E2E test_cache_flush.sh — write-behind cache deferred-touch timer verification","description":"Create a new E2E script verifying the write-behind cache (ReadCache) deferred-touch batching behavior. Currently there is ZERO E2E coverage for cache flush timing — the 30s batch flush and cache coherency are only tested in unit/stress tests.

BACKGROUND: The write-behind cache batches read-timestamp ('touched_at') updates into 30-second deferred flushes to reduce SQLite write pressure. Cache coherency is maintained via scope-fingerprinted keys and dual-indexing (slug+human_key, key+id).

TEST CASES TO IMPLEMENT:

1. cache_hit_after_first_read
   - Send a message, fetch inbox (cache miss), fetch inbox again (cache hit)
   - Assert: second fetch returns same data, server logs show cache hit
   - Measure: second fetch should be measurably faster (< 50% of first)

2. cache_invalidation_after_write
   - Fetch inbox (cached), send new message, fetch inbox again
   - Assert: third fetch includes new message (cache invalidated by write)

3. cache_scope_isolation
   - Create 2 projects, send messages to each, fetch inboxes
   - Assert: project A cache doesn't leak into project B responses

4. deferred_touch_timer_fires
   - Send messages, read them (triggers touched_at update)
   - Wait >35 seconds, query DB directly for touched_at timestamps
   - Assert: timestamps have been updated (deferred flush occurred)
   - NOTE: This is the critical test — verifies the 30s timer actually fires

5. cache_survives_rapid_reads
   - Send 50 rapid read requests for same inbox in <1 second
   - Assert: all return consistent data, server doesn't degrade
   - Assert: DB shows at most 2-3 write operations (batched), not 50

LOGGING: Full artifact capture per case. Include explicit timing measurements in timing.txt.
